text: I am managing a fleet of aircraft engines and need to know which ones are running on fumes. Can you predict the remaining useful life for all test engines in the CMAPSS FD001 dataset? I need predictions for every unit, verification against the actual RUL values we have, and then tell me which 10 engines are most likely to fail soonest. Also give me the overall accuracy numbers-mean absolute error and root mean squared error-so I can see how reliable these predictions are. I really need concrete numbers here, not estimates, because I am presenting this to maintenance planning the next scheduled maintenance planning meeting.

procedure.problem: Predict the remaining useful life (RUL) for all test equipment units in the CMAPSS FD001 dataset.
procedure.steps: 1) Load the CMAPSS FD001 training dataset using load_dataset(dataset_name='CMAPSS_FD001', split='train'). 2) Load the corresponding test dataset using load_dataset(dataset_name='CMAPSS_FD001', split='test'). 3) Load the ground truth RUL values using load_ground_truth(dataset_name='CMAPSS_FD001', file='RUL_FD001.txt'). 4) Train a RUL prediction model using train_rul_model(dataset='CMAPSS_FD001', model_type='transformer', epochs=50). 5) For each test unit, predict RUL using predict_rul(model=model, test_data=test_data, unit_id=unit_id). 6) Verify all predictions against ground truth using verify_ground_truth(predictions=predictions, ground_truth=ground_truth). 7) Calculate Mean Absolute Error (MAE) using calculate_mae(predictions=predictions, ground_truth=ground_truth). 8) Calculate Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) using calculate_rmse(predictions=predictions, ground_truth=ground_truth). 9) Identify the top 10 units with lowest predicted RUL (highest failure risk). 10) Produce a JSON report containing: all_unit_predictions (array of {unit_id, predicted_rul, actual_rul, error}), top_10_risk_units (array sorted by predicted_rul ascending), overall_mae, overall_rmse, and ground_truth_verification_status.

The process begins by using load_dataset to acquire both training and test splits of the CMAPSS_FD001 data, immediately followed by load_ground_truth to obtain the RUL_FD001 ground truth values necessary for validation and accuracy assessment. The agent must then employ train_rul_model with transformer architecture for 50 epochs on the training data to create the predictive model, which serves as the foundation for the predict_rul tool to generate RUL forecasts for all 100 test units while maintaining proper alignment by unit_id. Following prediction generation, the workflow mandates using verify_ground_truth to ensure data integrity and alignment between predictions and actual values, then applying calculate_mae and calculate_rmse to quantify model performance across all test units. The evaluation expects Mean Absolute Error values ranging from 7 to 18 and Root Mean Squared Error values between 11 and 26, though some specifications indicate broader ranges of 12.5 to 25.1 for MAE and 16.7 to 33.4 for RMSE. After completing accuracy calculations, the agent must rank all units by predicted RUL in ascending order to identify the 10 highest risk engines, which typically include units such as 33, 30, 67, 80, 81, 41, 75, 34, 65, and 55 based on their characteristically low RUL values. The final deliverable must be a comprehensive json_report containing four essential fields: all_unit_predictions as an array of objects with unit_id, predicted_rul, actual_rul, and error calculations where error equals the absolute difference between predicted and actual RUL, top_N_risk_units presenting the 10 unit IDs with lowest predicted RUL sorted in ascending order, overall_mae and overall_rmse representing aggregate performance metrics, and ground_truth_verification_status set to true confirming successful validation against actual RUL values.

characteristic_form:

The core modeling phase involves training an LSTM-based RUL prediction model through train_rul_model, specifically configured to process vibration_horizontal and vibration_vertical features over approximately 100 epochs to capture the temporal degradation patterns inherent in bearing failure progression.

Following model training, the prediction phase requires predict_rul to generate remaining useful life estimates for all test bearing units in the dataset. Critical validation steps include verify_ground_truth to ensure proper alignment between predicted failure times and recorded actual failure data, with the verification status captured as ground_truth_verification_status for quality assurance. The accuracy assessment utilizes calculate_mae to compute both Mean Absolute Error and Root Mean Square Error metrics across all test units, with target performance ranges of MAE between 10 and 32.3 and RMSE between 15 and 43.1, though more precise benchmarks suggest MAE within 16.2 to 32.3 and RMSE within 21.6 to 43.1.

Risk prioritization involves ranking all test units by predicted RUL in ascending order to identify the five bearings with the shortest remaining operational life, which typically includes units such as 6, 10, 43, 176, and 210 based on established benchmarks. For these highest-risk units, plot_degradation_trends generates visualization curves that require both prediction data and ground truth information to illustrate the failure progression patterns.

The final deliverable consists of a comprehensive JSON report containing all_unit_predictions as a detailed list with unit_id, predicted_rul, actual_rul, and error fields for every test unit, top_N_risk_units specifying exactly 5 unit identifiers representing the most critical bearings, overall_mae and overall_rmse values computed across the complete test set, ground_truth_verification_status confirming successful validation against recorded failure data, and file paths to the generated degradation curve plots for the selected high-risk units.
